Re:

Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
You have asked our advice on behalf of Margaret C. wright, 
one of the elected directors of the San Bernardino Valley 
Municipal Water District ("District"), with respect to her 
participation in the proposed letting of a contract for the 
construction of the Santa Ana River Crossing ("SARC") Pipeline. 
Our advice is based on the following facts supplied in your 
letter seeking advice and in our subsequent telephone 
conversation. 
The SARC pipeline, which the District is planning to 
construct, will cross property owned by the North Fork Water 
Company. 
The North Fork property is in a floodplain, and is 
presently used for provision of irrigation water to its 
stockholders. 
North Fork has no plans to change the use of this 
property. 
The SARC pipeline will cross the property, but will 
not be used for, or available for use for, supplying water to 
the property now served oy North Fork. 
The SARC pipeline will 
not affect the supply of water to the North Fork property. 
The 
pipeline will cross the North Fork property along an easement 
which has already been obtained by the District. 
As far as you 
know, the construction of the pipeline over the North Fork 
property will not affect the value of that property. 
Mrs. Wright and her husband have an investment interest of 
over $1,000 in North Fork. 
In addition, her husband is an 
officer of North Fork. 
Mrs. Wrignt disqualified herself from 
participating in the District vote on the easement over the 
North Fork property. 
She has now asked whether she can 
participate in future decisions concerning the contract for the 
SA..~C pipel ine. 


James Dilworth 
May 11, 1983 
Page 2 
The Political Reform Act prohibits an official from making 
or participating in a governmental decision in which she has a 
financial interest. 
Government Code Section 87100.l1 
An 
official has a financial interest ~n a decision if it is 
reasonably foreseeable that tne decision will have a material 
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public 
generally, on a ousiness entity in whicn the official has an 
investment of over $1,000. 
Section 87103. 
Since Mrs. Wright 
and her husband have an investment interest of over $1,000 in 
North Fork, she must disqualify herself from participating in 
any decLsion if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision 
will have a material financial effect on North Fork. 
From the facts that you have provided, it does not appear 
that the construction of tne SARC pipeline over the Nortn Fork 
property will have a material financial effect on the value of 
North Fork's property,ll or on its incomell or other 
assets. 
Therefore, it is our advice that the Political Reform 
Act does not require Mrs. Wright to disqualify herself from 
decisions on tne contract for construction of the pipeline. 
This letter does not constitute advice under Section 1090. 
Since Mrs. Wright and ner hUSband own over 3% of the stock of 
Nortn ForK, and ner husband is an officer of North Fork (see 
Section 1091.5), you may want to consider the possible 
application of Section 1090 to tnis situation. 
!I All statutory references are to the Government Code 
unless otherwise stated. 
21 
If any facts come to light wnich suggest that the 
pipeline will affect the value of the property, you should 
consult Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702 to 
determine wnether tne effect would be material within the 
meaning of the Act. 
II If any facts come to light whicn suggest that either 
the contract under consideration or the pipeline will affect 
North Fork's income, you should consult Commission regulation 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702 to determine whether the effect 
would be material within the meaning of tne Act. 


James Dilwortn

Dear Mr. Dilworth: 
April 8, 1983 
Subject: 
North Fork Property for SARC Pipeline 
As you have requested, I have reviewed the effect of the proposed SARC Pipeline 
on the property owned by the North Fork Water Company (San Bernardino County 
Parcel Nos. 297-061-8 and 297-061-12). 
These parcels are located south 
of Greenspot Road near the present terminus of the Foothill Pipeline. 
The 
SARC Pipeline is proposed to traverse the property from near the northeast 
corner to the southwest corner. 
The SARC Pipeline will have no operational effects on the North Fork Water 
Company as that Company currently has water delivery facilities in-place 
at the "North Fork Turnout." 
This turnout delivers State Project water 
to the existing Company facilities at the North Fork Canal. 
The presence 
of the SARC Pipeline does not make water service any more available than 
is currently available to the property. 
Water service would not be available from this line except near Greenspot 
Road. 
Water service from the District could be made available to the North 
Fork Water Company from the existing Foothill line. 
All water service should 
be located near public right-of-way. 
Water service that could come from 
the SARC Pipeline is at the same pressure and conditions as that which would 
come from the Foothill line. 
According to the San Bernardino Valley Water Conservation District, there 
are blanket easements to that District for water spreading, including the 
right to construct, maintain, operate and repair ponds, dikes, ditches, 
etc. 
Copies of these easements are attached for your review. 
Manager/Engineer

real property for the purpose of spreading such water so as to cause and per-

struct, maintain, operate and repair ponds, dikes, ditches, trenches, canals

embedding.first_500_words: P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 Technical Assistance (916) 322·5662 James Dilworth Attorney at Law 7172 Magnolia Avenue Riverside, CA 92504 Dear Mr. Dilworth: Administration 322-5660 Executive/legal 322-5901 May 11, 1983 Enforcement 322-64.41 Re: Advice No. A-83-118 Statements of Economic Interest 322-6444 You have asked our advice on behalf of Margaret C. wright, one of the elected directors of the San Bernardino Valley Municipal Water District ("District"), with respect to her participation in the proposed letting of a contract for the construction of the Santa Ana River Crossing ("SARC") Pipeline. Our advice is based on the following facts supplied in your letter seeking advice and in our subsequent telephone conversation. The SARC pipeline, which the District is planning to construct, will cross property owned by the North Fork Water Company. The North Fork property is in a floodplain, and is presently used for provision of irrigation water to its stockholders. North Fork has no plans to change the use of this property. The SARC pipeline will cross the property, but will not be used for, or available for use for, supplying water to the property now served oy North Fork. The SARC pipeline will not affect the supply of water to the North Fork property. The pipeline will cross the North Fork property along an easement which has already been obtained by the District. As far as you know, the construction of the pipeline over the North Fork property will not affect the value of that property. Mrs. Wright and her husband have an investment interest of over $1,000 in North Fork. In addition, her husband is an officer of North Fork. Mrs. Wrignt disqualified herself from participating in the District vote on the easement over the North Fork property. She has now asked whether she can participate in future decisions concerning the contract for the SA..~C pipel ine. James Dilworth May 11, 1983 Page 2 The Political Reform Act prohibits an official from making or participating in a governmental decision in which she has a financial interest. Government Code Section 87100.l1 An official has a financial interest ~n a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that tne decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on a ousiness entity in whicn the official has an investment of over $1,000. Section 87103. Since Mrs. Wright and her husband have an investment interest of over $1,000 in North Fork, she must disqualify herself from participating in any decLsion if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on North Fork. From the facts that you have provided, it does not appear that the construction of tne SARC pipeline over the Nortn Fork property will have a material financial effect on the value of North Fork's property,ll or on its incomell or other assets. Therefore, it is our advice that the Political Reform Act does not require Mrs. Wright to disqualify herself from
